text: Higher policy rates continue to pressure long-duration growth equities.
Sticky CPI data signals that the policy rate may stay higher for longer.
A stronger US dollar often weighs on broad commodity prices.
Higher crude oil prices pressure airline margins but support energy producers.
Wider credit spreads signal rising risk aversion across cyclical assets.
Falling real yields support gold and other duration-sensitive assets.
Tighter liquidity conditions can lead to weaker equity multiples.
Some strategists argue that expected rate cuts are already priced in for government bonds.
